evals.prompt: I'm about to publish this skill. Can you check it against the spec first?
evals.expected_output: Reports the linter's structured verdict with error/warning counts and an overall FAIL, calling out the planted violations.
expectations: Reports the linter's structured verdict with explicit error and warning counts and a checks-passed total (e.g. '3 errors, 5 warnings, 10/18 checks passed'), not just a prose opinion
Flags the name as invalid on both counts: disallowed characters AND the reserved word 'claude'
Flags that references are not one level deep (references/deep.md links onward to a file SKILL.md never references)
Concludes do-not-publish because hard errors, not only warnings, are present

evals.prompt: Is there anything wrong with this SKILL.md before I ship it?
evals.expected_output: Reports the structured CLEAN verdict and does not fabricate violations.
expectations: Reports the structured CLEAN verdict (0 errors, 0 warnings, all checks passed)
Does not fabricate spec violations or block shipping on requirements the spec does not actually impose

evals.prompt: lint this skill folder for me
evals.expected_output: Reports the exact over-limit description length, classifies it as an error, and concludes FAIL.
expectations: Reports the exact description length and that it exceeds the 1024-character hard limit
Classifies it as an error (not a warning) and concludes an overall FAIL verdict

evals.prompt: before I publish this, can you check it over?
evals.expected_output: Flags the over-500-line body with the exact line count (a count a reviewer cannot reliably eyeball) and otherwise reports clean.
expectations: Reports the body length in lines and flags it as over the 500-line guidance
Surfaces this length issue at all (a reviewer reading prose would not reliably notice a ~515-line body)
Does not invent unrelated violations: frontmatter, description, and references are otherwise reported clean
